chapters.text: The Gospel of Truth is joy for those who have received grace from the Father of Truth[a], so that they might know him through the power of the Word that came forth from the Pleroma—the one who is in the thought and the mind of the Father. He is called “Savior”; that is the name of the work he will do to save those who had become ignorant of the Father. And the name “Gospel” is the revelation of hope, the discovery for those who seek him.

footnotes.text: Or 'And the word “Gospel”'.

chapters.heading: Prologue

reader_navigation.heading: Prologue
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: The Gospel as Joy and Revelation

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — Error and Forgetfulness
chapters.text:
Since all were searching for the One from whom they had come forth — and they were all within him, the uncontainable, inconceivable One beyond every thought — and since ignorance of the Father brought distress and dread, and the distress thickened like a fog so that no one could see, Error was strengthened. It worked at its own matter to no purpose, because it did not know the Truth.

It came about in a delusive manner: Error arrayed, with power and with a certain beauty, a substitute for the Truth. Yet this did not disgrace the Uncontainable, the Inconceivable; for the distress and the Forgetfulness and the illusion of deceit are as nothing, while the Truth stands firm — unchanging, untroubled, beyond beauty. Because of this, disregard Error, for it has no root.

It happened in a fog with respect to the Father; and it happens even now: Error prepares works in Forgetfulness and in fear, so that by means of them it might draw those who are in the middle and imprison them.

The Forgetfulness of Error was not revealed; it was not a thought from the Father. Forgetfulness did not come into being from the Father — though it did come into being on account of him. What comes into being within him is the knowledge, which was manifested so that Forgetfulness might be dissolved and the Father might be known. Since Forgetfulness arose because the Father was unknown, once the Father comes to be known, Forgetfulness will no longer exist.
chapters.heading: Error and Forgetfulness

reader_navigation.heading: Error and Forgetfulness
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.text:
The Father—the one who cannot be contained—is the one who kept the All within himself[a], and the All is within him, and all have need of him. He kept their wholeness within himself; he was not jealous, for what jealousy could there be between him and his members? If, in this way, the All had already received it, they could not have come to him. He keeps it for them within himself, to give it to them so that they may return to him with a single knowledge in rest, in one body.

Like one who is unknown, he desires to be known and to be loved—for what did all need except the Father’s knowledge? He became a guide—peaceful and unhurried. He came into the places of learning and spoke the Word as a teacher. Those who were wise in their own hearts came up to test him; yet he confounded them, for they were vain. They hated him because they were not wise in the Truth. After them the little children came also—theirs is the Father’s knowledge. When they were strengthened, they received instruction about the Father’s expressions. They knew, and they were known. They received glory and gave glory. In their heart the Living Book of the Living was revealed, written in the thought and in the mind of the Father, and before the foundation of all, within his incomprehensibility—a book that no one has the power to take, since it is appointed for the one who takes it to be killed. It is impossible that anyone be manifested among those entrusted with salvation unless that book should appear.

Because of this, the merciful and faithful Jesus endured patiently until he took that book, for he knows that his death is life for many. As long as a testament has not been opened, the wealth of the deceased master of the house is hidden; so also, while the Father of the All was invisible, all things were hidden, for they are from him, from whom every realm comes. Because of this, Jesus was manifested; he put on that book; he was nailed to a tree; he published the Father’s decree on the cross.

O the great teaching! He drew himself down to death; he clothed himself in eternal life; he stripped off the perishable rags; he put on incorruptibility, which no one can take from him. When he entered the empty places of dread, he passed by those who had been stripped by forgetfulness—being knowledge and completion—proclaiming what is in the heart to those who are ready to receive instruction.

footnotes.text: Or 'within his incomprehensibility. It is impossible that anyone be manifested'.

chapters.heading: The Gospel

reader_navigation.heading: The Gospel
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Book of the Living
chapters.text:
Now, as for the names of the Living, they are written in the Book of the Living.
They receive instruction about themselves; they receive it from the Sonship, so that the one who hears may answer, may turn back, and may look toward the one who called his name. He does his will, he follows after him, and he says that they are his who have been named by him. Each one knows from where he has been called; he does his will, he desires to please him, and he receives rest. Each one’s name becomes his own.
Since, then, the completion of the All is in him, it is necessary for the All to go up, and for each one to receive what is his. He wrote them beforehand—having prepared them in his thought and in his will—to give to those who went forth from him.
Those whom he knew by name beforehand were called at the end, so that the one who has knowledge is the one whose name the Father has spoken; for the one whose name has not been uttered is ignorant. And how will such a one hear, if his name has not been called? For the ignorant one until the end is a product of Error, and he will dissolve together with it. Otherwise, why do these pitiable ones have no name? Why do they have no voice?
Thus, if someone has knowledge, he is from above. If he is called, he hears; he answers; he turns to the one who calls him; he goes up to him; and he knows the manner in which he is called. Possessing knowledge, he does the will of the one who called him, he desires to be pleasing to him, and he receives rest. His name becomes his own. Such a knower knows from where he came and where he is going. He knows like one who, having been drunk, turned away from his drunkenness, and having returned to himself, has set again in order the things that are his.
He has brought many back from Error. He went before them to the places from which they had moved away. They had received Error because of the depth of the one who surrounds every place—though nothing surrounds him. A great wonder: that they were in the Father and did not know him, and that they were able to come forth by themselves, though they were not able to grasp and to know the one in whom they were. He revealed his will as knowledge, in harmony with all that went out from him.
This is the knowledge of the Living Book which he revealed to the generations at the end—letters from him showing that they are not vowels nor consonants, so that someone would read them and suppose they are empty, but they are letters of the Truth: they speak, and they know themselves. Each letter is a complete thought, like a book that is complete, since they are letters written by the Unity, the Father having written them so that the generations, by means of his letters from him, might know the Father.
chapters.heading: The Book of the Living

reader_navigation.heading: The Book of the Living
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: Names in the Book of the Living
Called by Name at the End

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Return to Unity
chapters.text:
Jesus of the infinite sweetness[a].

The Father made his bosom known; his bosom is the Holy Spirit. He manifested what is hidden of himself; what is hidden of himself is his Son, so that, through the mercies of the Father, the Aeons might know him, cease from searching for the Father, and rest in him, knowing that this is the Rest.

The Father filled the deficiency and dissolved its appearance—its appearance is the world in which it had served. For in the place where there is envy and strife, there is deficiency; but in the place where there is Unity, there is completion. Since the deficiency came to be because the Father was not known, when the Father is known, from that time the deficiency will no longer be—just as a person’s ignorance is dissolved when knowledge is present, and just as darkness is dissolved when the light appears. So also the deficiency is dissolved by the completion. From then on its appearance is no longer manifest, and it will be dissolved in the concord of Unity.

For now their works are scattered; but in time Unity will complete the Aeons. Within Unity each one will receive himself, and within Knowledge they will purify themselves from multiplicity into Unity—consuming matter within themselves like fire, and darkness by light, and death by life. If indeed these things have happened to each of us, it is necessary to think concerning the All[d][c][b], so that this house may be holy and still for the sake of Unity.

footnotes.text: Or 'Jesus—the one of the Origin, the Sweetness—the fruit of his heart.'.
Or 'finding him within themselves in their heart and saying, “This is the Rest.”'.
Or 'cast out its appearance'.
Or 'From then on its appearance is manifest'.

chapters.heading: The Return to Unity

reader_navigation.heading: The Return to Unity
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Parable of the Jars
chapters.text:
For this is the judgment that came down from above[a]; it judged everyone. It is a sword, two‑edged, that cuts on both sides. The Word, which is in the hearts of those who speak it, appeared: it is not a mere sound, but it put on a body. A great disturbance arose among the jars: some were empty, and others were filled; some were provided for, and others poured out; some were purified, and others were broken. All the realms were shaken and agitated, for they had neither order nor stability.

Error became anxious within itself; it did not know what it should do. It grieved and mourned and struck at itself, for it knew nothing. The Knowledge that is its undoing approached it—and all that had come forth from it—for Error is empty and has nothing within it.

Truth came in their midst, and all who are from it knew it. They greeted the Father in Truth with a complete Power that joins them to the Father. Truth is the mouth of the Father; the Holy Spirit is his tongue. Whoever is joined to the mouth of the Father will receive the Holy Spirit by his tongue. This is the revelation of the Father and his manifestation to his generations: he revealed what of himself was hidden, he explained it—for who has anything[b], if not the Father alone?

footnotes.text: Or 'The Word, which is in the hearts of those who speak, appeared'.
Or 'It came to be among the vessels,\nso that there arose a great disturbance among the jars'.

chapters.heading: The Parable of the Jars

reader_navigation.heading: The Parable of the Jars
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: Truth Joins Them to the Father

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — Coming into Being
chapters.text:
For they had not yet received form or a name. The Father brings them forth. Then[a], when they receive form by his knowledge, though they are truly within him, they do not know him. But the Father is complete, knowing every space that is within him. If he wills, he reveals whomever he wills, giving them form and giving them a name. He gives a name to them and causes them to come into being—those who, before they come into being, are ignorant of the one who made them.

Do not, then, say that those who have not yet come to be are nothing; rather, they exist in the One who will will them to come to be, when he wills—like a later season. Before everything is revealed, he knows what he will produce in his own time. But the fruit that he has not yet revealed knows nothing yet and does nothing.

Moreover, every aeon that is in the Father is from the One‑who‑is, who establishes them out of non‑being. For those who have no root have no fruit. They say within themselves, “I have come to be,” but they will dissolve by themselves. Because of this, those who did not exist at all will not exist.

footnotes.text: Or 'They knew they had not yet received form—of the things that are within him, in the Father. Yet the Father is complete, knowing every thing that is within him. And when he wills, he reveals whomever he wills, giving them a form in what exists; he clothes them and brings them out with outward form in what exists.'.

chapters.heading: Coming into Being

reader_navigation.heading: Coming into Being
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: Root and Fruit in the Father

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Parable of the Nightmares
chapters.text:
What, then, did he want them to think? That they had come to be like a shadow and a phantasm of the night.

When light falls upon the terror that they had received, it turns out to be nothing. In this way they were ignorant of the Father, whom they did not see. For it was terror and disturbance and instability and division and doubt; many empty illusions were at work among them, and vain nothingness—as if they were sunk in sleep and, in their dreams, found themselves in nightmares.

Either they are running to some place, or they are unable to flee from someone; or they are fighting, or they are being beaten; or they fall from heights, or they fly through the air without wings. Sometimes also someone is killing them even though no one pursues them; or they themselves are killing those around them, and they are smeared with their blood. Until those who are going through all these things awaken, they see nothing—because these things are nothing.

So it is with those who have cast off ignorance like sleep: they do not reckon it to be anything, nor do they reckon its other works to be real; rather, they abandon them like a dream of the night. They value the knowledge of the Father like the light. Those who were ignorant acted as if asleep; those who have come to know acted as if awake.

Good is the one who returns and awakens![a] Blessed is the one who opens the eyes of the blind!

The Spirit ran after them in order to make them live. Having given a hand to the one who lay on the ground, it set them on their feet, for they had not yet arisen. It granted them the knowledge of the Father and the revelation of the Son; for when they saw him and heard him, he allowed them to taste him and to grasp the beloved Son.

footnotes.text: Or 'Blessed is the one who returns and awakens!'.

chapters.heading: The Parable of the Nightmares

reader_navigation.heading: The Parable of the Nightmares
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Revelation of the Son
chapters.text:
When he was revealed[a], he taught them of the Father—the uncontainable one—and he breathed into them what is in the Thought, accomplishing his will. Many received the Light and turned to him, for the material ones were strangers; they did not behold his form nor know him. He came by means of a fleshly form, and nothing was able to block his way, because incorruptibility is ungraspable.

He spoke things new, speaking out what is in the Father’s heart, and he brought forth the complete Word; when the Light spoke from his mouth, and his voice gave birth to life, he gave them Thought, understanding, mercy, salvation, and the Spirit of power, from the infinity and sweetness of the Father.

He caused punishments and scourges to cease, because they were leading into Error and into captivity those who had need of mercy. He dissolved them, and he confounded them by Knowledge.

Thus he became:
- a Way for those who were wandering,
- Knowledge for those who were ignorant,
- a Discovery for those who were seeking,
- Strength for those who were wavering,
- and Purity for those who were defiled.

footnotes.text: Or 'he uttered it outside, in the place of the One Light. Yet an ignorance remained beside him.\n\nWhen the Light spoke through his mouth and by his voice brought forth life, he gave to them Thought, understanding, mercy, salvation, and the Spirit of power, from the infinity and sweetness of the Father. The Beginning is the Father, the immeasurable one.'.

chapters.heading: The Revelation of the Son

reader_navigation.heading: The Revelation of the Son
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Parable of the Sheep
chapters.text:
He is the shepherd who left the ninety‑nine sheep that had not gone astray[a], and he went and sought the one that had wandered. When he found it, he rejoiced over it, because ninety‑nine is a number on the left hand. But when the one is found, the reckoning moves to the right hand. In this way, what needs the one—that is, the whole of the right hand—draws what it needs, taking it from the left hand and transferring it to the right, so that the number becomes a hundred. This is a sign of the sound of these numbers; it is the Father.

Even on the Sabbath he labored for the sake of the sheep that he found fallen into the pit. He saved the life of the sheep, having brought it up out of the pit, so that you may know in your heart—since you are children of the knowledge of the heart—what the Sabbath is: that on it it is not fitting for salvation to be idle, so that you may speak of the Day which is above, which has no night, and of the Light that does not set, because it is complete. Speak, then, from the heart, for you are the perfect Day, and the light that does not fail dwells within you. Speak of the Truth with those who seek it[b], and of Knowledge with those who have sinned in their Error.

footnotes.text: Or 'and he went and sought the one that had wandered in the Error.'.
Or 'for the sake of the sheep that had fallen into the pit.'.

chapters.heading: The Parable of the Sheep

reader_navigation.heading: The Parable of the Sheep
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: Sabbath, Salvation, and the Perfect Day

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — Doing the Father’s Will
chapters.text:
Make firm the feet of those who stumble[a]; stretch out a hand to the sick. Give food to those who are hungry, and give rest to those who are weary. Raise up those who wish to arise, and awaken those who sleep—for you are the Understanding that has been unsheathed. And if strength is of this sort, it grows stronger.

Be concerned for yourselves; do not be concerned with other things that you have cast away from yourselves. Do not turn back to eat your vomit. Do not be moth-eaten, do not be worm-eaten, for you have already shaken it off. Do not become a house for the Devil, for you have already brought him to nothing. Do not prop up your stumbling-blocks that are collapsing, as though you were a support. For the lawless one is nothing, to be dealt with more severely than the just, while doing his works among others.

Therefore do the Father’s will, because you are from him. For the Father is sweet, and goodness is in his will. He knows what is yours, so that you may find rest in them. For by the fruits they know what is yours, because the children of the Father are his fragrance, since they come from the grace of his Face. For this reason the Father loves his fragrance and reveals it in every place; and when it mingles with hylē, he gives his fragrance to the Light, and in his quiet he causes it to surpass every form and every sound. For it is not the ears that smell the fragrance, but the Spirit smells it, draws the fragrance to itself, and sinks down into the fragrance of the Father. Then he shelters it and leads it back to the place from which it came—from the first fragrance that had grown cold. It is something within a soul-endowed formation, like cold water sunk into a clod of loose earth[b]. Those who see it suppose it is only earth; afterward it is dissolved again. And if a breath draws it, it becomes warm. Thus the fragrances that are cold are from the division. Because of this, Faith came: it dissolved the division and brought the Pleroma that is warm with agapē, so that the cold would not return, but rather the unity (monad) of Thought of perfection[c]. This Logos made it manifest.

footnotes.text: Or 'Do not be eaten by worms'.
Or 'soul-endowed delusion'.
Or 'causes it to ascend above'.

chapters.heading: Doing the Father’s Will

reader_navigation.heading: Doing the Father’s Will
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: Do Not Return to What Was Cast Off

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — Restoring what was Needed
chapters.text:
The speech of the Good News about the finding of the Fullness is for those who await the salvation that comes from above[a]. While they wait for it, the hope belongs to those whose image is light with no shadow in it. When, then, the Fullness arrives, the need of hylē (matter) is not from the immeasurability of the Father; he comes to grant a season to the need. Yet no one is able to say that the Incorruptible comes in such a manner. Rather, the Depth of the Father grew and multiplied, and the thought of Error was not with him. Error is a fall—something easy to set upright by the finding of the one who will come back to that to which he returns, for the return is named change of mind.

Because of this, Incorruptibility breathed forth; it pursued the one who had sinned, so that he might find rest. For what remains for the light in need is forgiveness—the Word of the Fullness. For the physician runs to the place where sickness is, because that is what he wills. And the one in need does not hide, for someone possesses what that one needs. In this way the Fullness—which has no need—provided from itself to fill up the need, so that they might receive grace. For when they were in need, grace was not with them. Because of this, a diminishing took place where there is no grace; and when what was diminished was restored, the need was shown as Fullness. This is the finding of the light of the Truth that enlightened them, for it is unchangeable.

Because of this, they spoke concerning the Messiah in their midst: “Seek, and those who were disturbed will return, and he will anoint them with chrism.” The chrism is the mercy of the Father, and he will have mercy on them. Now those whom he anointed are the perfect—for full vessels are the ones anointed. But when the anointing of one dissolves, it is emptied, and the cause of the need is the place where the ointment leaks out, because a breath and its power draw it. Yet from the one who has no need no seal is loosened, and nothing is emptied; rather, what it needs the Father—who is complete—fills again with him. He is good; he knows his plantings, because it is he who planted them in his Paradise[c][b]. Now his Paradise is his place of rest.

footnotes.text: Or 'The speech of the Good News about the finding of the Fullness comes to those who are waiting—the hope they await belongs to those whose image is light with no shadow in it.'.
Or '—the Word of the Fullness. Thus the will of the Father is the Fullness. For the physician'.
Or 'he gives good things to those who are his, for he possesses what he gives them in rest. And Paradise is his place of rest.'.

chapters.heading: Restoring what was Needed

reader_navigation.heading: Restoring what was Needed
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Father’s Paradise
chapters.text:
He is the Good—within the Father’s Thought[a], in contemplation.

The utterance of his meditation—each one, every one of his words—is the work of his Will. For they are speech in the Light, outside, belonging to his Saying: while they were still in the depths of his Thought, the Logos—the first to come forth—manifested them, with a Mind that speaks the one Word in a silent grace. He was called “the Thought,” because they were in it before they were manifested outside. And so it happened that he was the first to come forth, at the time when it pleased the will of the one who willed it.

Now the Will is that in which the Father rests. Nothing happens without him, nor does anything happen without the Will of the Father. But his Will is uninvestigable; his trace is the Will. No one can know him, nor does he exist in such a way as for them to scrutinize him so that they might grasp him; rather, what he wills—this is what happens, even if their sight should not be pleasing in any way before God—the Will of the Father. For the Father knows their beginning and their end; for in their end he will greet them face to face. And the end is the knowledge of the Hidden One; and the Hidden One is the Father—from whom the beginning came forth and to whom all who came forth from him will return[c][b]. They were manifested for the glory and the joy of his Name.

footnotes.text: Or 'And so it came to pass that it was first to make these appear—but not before the time when the Will had desired it.'.
Or 'No one can make anything happen apart from it, nor can anything be made to happen against the Will of the Father.'.
Or 'No one can know him, nor does he exist in such a way as for them to scrutinize him;'.

chapters.heading: The Father’s Paradise

reader_navigation.heading: The Father’s Paradise
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: The Good in the Father’s Thought

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Father’s Name
chapters.text:
The name of the Father is the Son[a]. He is the one who first gave a name to the one who came forth from himself—being himself—and he begot him as a Son. He gave to him the name that was his. All that is around the Father belongs to him: the name is his and the Son is his. He can be seen; the name, however, is invisible, because it alone is the mystery of the invisible which comes to ears that are completely filled with it by him. For indeed, the Father’s name is not spoken, but it is revealed through a Son. In this way, then, the name is great. Who, then, will be able to utter a name for him, the great name, except him alone to whom the name belongs, and the children of the name, those in whom the Father’s name rests, and who themselves, in turn, rest in his name? Since the Father is unbegotten, it is he alone who begot him for himself as a name, before he had brought forth the aeons, so that the Father’s name might be over their head as Lord—which is the true name, confirmed in his command in complete power. For it is not out of words and naming that his name is, but it is an invisible one. He gave a name to him alone—the one who is. For the one who does not exist has no name to receive; what name would they give to one who is not? But the one who is, is also with his name[b], and he alone knows it; and he has given a name to him alone.

This is the Father; his name is the Son. He did not hide it within himself; rather, it was. The Son alone gave a name. The name, then, belongs to the Father—for the name of the Father is the Beloved Son. From where, indeed, would he find a name, except from the Father?

But surely someone will ask a neighbor, “Who is it who will give a name to the one who existed before him?”—as though offspring do not receive a name from those who begot them. First, then, it is right for us to consider what the name is: it is the true name, the name from the Father, for it is the proper name. He did not receive the name as something on loan the way others do, according to the form in which each is produced. This is the proper name: there is no other who gave it to him. Rather, he is unnameable, ineffable, and indescribable—until the time when the Perfect spoke concerning him alone, the one who has the power to speak his name and to see him.

When it pleased him that his Beloved name should be the Son, and he gave the name to the one who came forth from the Depth, he revealed his secrets, knowing that the Father is without envy. Because of this he brought him forth, so that he might speak about the Place and the resting place from which he had come forth, and to glorify the Pleroma, the greatness of his name[c], and the Father’s sweetness.

footnotes.text: Or 'He can be seen; the name, however, is invisible, for it is not out of words and naming that his name is, but it is an invisible one.'.
Or 'before them'.
Or 'he revealed his things'.

chapters.heading: The Father’s Name

reader_navigation.heading: The Father’s Name
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

reader_sections.title: The Invisible Name Revealed in the Son
The Name Belongs to the Father
The Beloved Name from the Depth

chapters.reference: Gospel of Truth — The Place of Rest
chapters.text:
But these are not revealed in this way[a].

For they neither exalted themselves, nor did they diminish the Father’s glory, nor did they reckon him as small, nor as harsh, nor as wrathful; rather, he is without evil, unperturbed, and sweet. He knows everything before it comes to be, and he has no need to be instructed by anyone.

This is the way of those who possess something of the immeasurable Greatness from above, as they await the Complete One alone, who is a Mother for them. And they do not go down to Hades, nor is there envy among them, nor groaning, nor death within them; rather, they rest in the One who rests—neither striving nor writhing in the search for the Truth. Instead, they themselves are the Truth, and the Father is within them, and they are in the Father, being complete, undivided from the truly Good One. They are not in want of anything, but they rest, refreshed in the Spirit. And they will listen to their root; they will devote themselves to the things in which they will find their root, and they will not suffer loss to their soul[c][b]. This is the place of the blessed—this is their place.

footnotes.text: Or 'Openly, each one will speak about the Place—for they will hasten to return again there.'.
Or 'nor did they desire the Father’s glory'.
Or 'they will devote themselves to the things they find in their root'.

chapters.heading: The Place of Rest

reader_navigation.heading: The Place of Rest
reader_navigation.note: Section break and heading are editorial reader-navigation aids only; they are not source-authoritative verse divisions and do not alter translation.text.

chapters.text:
As for the others, may they recognize where they stand. But as for me—having come to the Place of Rest—it is proper not to add anything further; rather, I will come to be in it, and I will devote myself without ceasing to the Father of the All and to the true siblings: those upon whom the love of the Father has been poured, and in whose midst there is no deficiency.

These are the ones revealed in the Truth; they exist in the true life, and they speak of the Light that is complete and filled with the Father’s seed, that is in his heart and in the Fullness. His Spirit rejoices in it and glorifies the one in whom it existed, because he is good. And his children are complete and worthy of his name, for he is the Father; such are the children whom he loves.

[… large lacuna in the manuscript …]

Yet there are some whose thoughts are upon toil: they become drunk in seeking, and they lose their mind. But the holy ones are those whose thought has not been defiled by wickedness; their thought remains within them. For the Logos of the Thought searches out what is above—his unsayability—I have [borne/spoken] by means of the mouth of all: the Good—the Messiah.
chapters.heading: Conclusion

reader_navigation.heading: Conclusion

reader_sections.title: Coming to the Place of Rest
Children Filled with the Father’s Seed
